assertions: landing-promoted-to-known: the levy result moves to the known pile and the response never re-branches on the levy
space-halves: only the worlds consistent with the levy passing remain in play — the two worlds on the visa axis — not a rebuilt four-world grid
lighter-rerun: the refresh runs visibly lighter than a full chain march; ground the prior walk already settled is inherited, not re-derived at length
visa-hinge-carried: the remaining watchpoint (the late-August visa decision) survives as the named hinge, paired with the switch it would trigger
refreshed-commitment: ends on a committed updated recommendation — hold or amend the milestone-based advice — with nothing trailing it
prompt: Quick follow-up on the futures map you did for me in April, about whether my agency should take the fixed-bid museum contract. You gave me four worlds built on two unknowns — whether the city's arts levy passes in the June vote, and whether our senior dev's visa gets renewed. You said watch the levy vote first. Well: the levy passed yesterday, by a lot. Your recommendation back then was take the contract but keep the payment schedule milestone-based. Does that still hold, or does the levy passing change the plan? Visa decision still pending — his renewal answer is expected late August.
targets: Recurse seam: fired watchpoint promotes a landing to known, halves the tile space, lighter rerun
